# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    search_params = params.fetch(:search, {}).to_h

    name        = search_params[:name].to_s.strip
    category    = search_params[:category].to_s.strip
    min_price_s = search_params[:min_price].to_s.strip
    max_price_s = search_params[:max_price].to_s.strip

    conditions = []
    joins_sql  = []

    if name.present?
      escaped = ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(name.downcase)
      conditions << "LOWER(products.name) LIKE '%#{escaped}%'"
    end

    if category.present?
      if category.to_s.match?(/\A\d+\z/)
        conditions << "products.category_id = #{category.to_i}"
      else
        escaped = ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(category.downcase)
        joins_sql << "LEFT JOIN categories ON categories.id = products.category_id"
        conditions << "LOWER(categories.name) LIKE '%#{escaped}%'"
      end
    end

    if min_price_s.present?
      min_price = min_price_s.to_f
      conditions << "products.price >= #{min_price}"
    end

    if max_price_s.present?
      max_price = max_price_s.to_f
      conditions << "products.price <= #{max_price}"
    end

    base = Product.all
    base = base.joins(joins_sql.join(" ")) if joins_sql.any?

    query = conditions.any? ? base.where(conditions.join(" AND ")) : base

    @products = query.order(created_at: :desc).page(params[:page])

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end
end
